statement.focus_question: \]In particular, is it true that\[\min I =2-(1+o(1))\frac{1}{n}?
statement.normalized:
For $x_1,\ldots,x_n\in [-1,1]$ let\[l_k(x)= rac{\prod_{i
eq k}(x-x_i)}{\prod_{i
eq k}(x_k-x_i)},\]which are such that $l_k(x_k)=1$ and $l_k(x_i)=0$ for $i
eq k$. What is the minimal value of\[I(x_1,\ldots,x_n)=\int_{-1}^1 \sum_k \lvert l_k(x)vert^2\mathrm{d}x?\]In particular, is it true that\[\min I =2-(1+o(1)) rac{1}{n}?\]
statement.original:
For $x_1,\ldots,x_n\in [-1,1]$ let\[l_k(x)= rac{\prod_{i
eq k}(x-x_i)}{\prod_{i
eq k}(x_k-x_i)},\]which are such that $l_k(x_k)=1$ and $l_k(x_i)=0$ for $i
eq k$.

What is the minimal value of\[I(x_1,\ldots,x_n)=\int_{-1}^1 \sum_k \lvert l_k(x)vert^2\mathrm{d}x?\]In particular, is it true that\[\min I =2-(1+o(1)) rac{1}{n}?\]
